Use Case Guide

Construction Software for Tenant Improvement Projects

TI work has unique demands: occupied buildings, landlord coordination, tight schedules, and TI allowance accounting. Here's the software that handles the nuances of commercial fit-out work.

✓ Updated April 2026

Tenant improvement (TI) contractors face a specific set of challenges that general-purpose construction software doesn't always handle well: working in occupied buildings with tenant and building management coordination requirements, managing TI allowances (money the landlord provides to fund improvements), navigating building security protocols and elevator scheduling, and often working on compressed schedules where the tenant has a hard business opening date.

The right construction software for TI work needs to handle commercial project management, AIA billing, subcontractor coordination, change orders, and the client communication that keeps tenants and landlords aligned during active construction.

What makes TI projects different

Multiple stakeholder management

TI projects typically involve three or more parties with different interests: the tenant (who wants fast completion, minimum disruption, maximum TI allowance usage), the landlord (who wants code compliance, no damage to base building, and liability protection), and often an architect and owner's representative. Managing communication and approvals across this group requires clear documentation trails.

TI allowance accounting

Tenant improvement allowances are landlord-funded budgets that cover some or all of the improvement costs. Tracking TI allowance usage — what's covered, what the tenant pays over the allowance, and how change orders affect the allowance balance — requires budget management that distinguishes between TI-funded and tenant-funded costs. Not all construction software handles this distinction cleanly.

Working in occupied buildings

Elevator booking, after-hours work requirements, dust containment, noise restrictions, and tenant neighbor notifications are all coordination tasks that require documented scheduling and communication. Software that supports detailed scheduling notes and can generate professional coordination letters and notices simplifies this administrative work.

Compressed schedules

TI projects are often on tenant opening deadlines that cannot move — the lease starts on a specific date regardless of construction status. Schedule management with tight dependencies, daily progress tracking, and early warning when work is falling behind is critical.

Top platforms for TI contractors

Procore — best for larger commercial TI work

Procore handles the full commercial TI workflow: drawing management, RFI routing to the tenant's architect, submittal management for landlord-required approvals, subcontractor contract management with standard commercial payment terms, and AIA billing. The budget module can be configured to track TI allowance separately from tenant-direct costs, creating the allowance balance reporting that landlords and tenants both need.

For TI contractors working on larger projects — $1M+ commercial fit-outs — Procore's documentation depth and owner access features support the multiple-stakeholder management that TI work requires. Landlords and tenant representatives can have project access calibrated to what each party needs to see.

Autodesk Build — best for design-build TI work

For TI contractors who also handle design (or who work closely with the architect during construction), Autodesk Build's connection to the Autodesk design ecosystem reduces coordination friction. When the architect revises drawings during construction — common on TI projects as tenant requirements evolve — updated sheets propagate to the field team automatically. Issue tracking linked to drawing locations makes RFI and coordination requests faster to resolve.

Autodesk Build — Drawing-linked field management for design-build TI contractors.

Explore Autodesk Build →

Buildertrend — best for smaller TI and light commercial work

For TI contractors working on smaller projects — retail fit-outs, small office renovations, restaurant build-outs under $500K — Buildertrend provides sufficient commercial functionality at a more accessible price point than Procore. Scheduling, change orders, subcontractor management, and basic job cost tracking are all included.

Buildertrend's client portal works well for TI projects — tenants can view project progress, approve change orders, and communicate with the construction team without email chains. The mobile app lets the project manager update status from the site, keeping the tenant informed in real time.

Buildertrend — Commercial TI management at a price point accessible for smaller contractors.

Try Buildertrend →

Knowify — best for specialty TI subcontractors

For electrical, HVAC, plumbing, and other specialty subcontractors working on TI projects, Knowify's job costing, AIA billing, and subcontractor management tools are well-suited to the commercial subcontractor workflow. AIA payment applications (G702/G703 format), retainage tracking, and lien waiver management are all supported — the standard billing requirements for commercial TI work.

Key software features for TI projects

AIA billing and payment applications

Commercial TI work is billed using AIA-format payment applications (G702/G703), not simple invoices. Your software needs to generate payment applications that match this format, track the schedule of values, handle retainage (typically 5–10% on commercial work), and maintain an accurate billing history for each contract. This is a commercial-specific feature that residential platforms don't always support.

Subcontractor management at scale

TI projects involve multiple specialty subs — typically 8–15 subcontractors on a commercial fit-out. Managing sub agreements, tracking sub billing relative to their contracts, and generating lien waivers as payments are made requires a structured subcontractor management system. Software that handles this automatically reduces administrative overhead and payment disputes.

Change order documentation

TI projects generate frequent change orders as tenant requirements evolve after construction starts. Every change needs a clear change event record (what triggered it), a cost impact (broken down by trade), the tenant's approval, and an updated budget. Platforms that make change order documentation fast and trackable keep this process from becoming administrative chaos.

Building code compliance tracking

TI projects require permits, inspections, and code compliance documentation. Your software should track inspection milestones, store permit documents, and create a compliance record that supports both certificate of occupancy filing and future tenant/landlord reference.

Schedule management with hard deadlines

TI schedules often work backward from a tenant opening date. Software that supports backward scheduling (calculating when work must start to meet a hard completion date) and shows daily critical path items helps project managers keep pace on compressed timelines.

Selecting software for your TI business

The right software for TI work depends on your average project size and the complexity of your stakeholder relationships:

  • Small TI (under $500K): Buildertrend or JobTread provide the right balance of features and price
  • Mid-size TI ($500K–$5M): Procore or Autodesk Build provide the commercial documentation depth these projects require
  • Specialty TI subs: Knowify for job costing and AIA billing; Fieldwire for drawing and task management on site
  • Design-build TI: Autodesk Build for design-construction integration; Houzz Pro for smaller design-build fit-outs

Scaling your TI business? Buildertrend handles the project management side, and Houzz Pro works well for design-build TI. Both offer free trials.

Try Buildertrend →   Try Houzz Pro →
